title: Elementary Mathematics I
description: Elementary set theory, subsets, and union, intersection, complements, and Venn diagrams. Real numbers, integers, rational and irrational numbers, mathematical induction, real sequences and series, theory of quadratic equations, binomial theorem. Complex numbers; algebra of complex numbers, the Argand diagram. De-Moivre’s theorem, nth roots of unity. Circular measure, trigonometric functions of angles of any magnitude, addition, and factor formulae.

quizzes.text: What is the value of log₁₀(1000)?
In set theory, A ∪ B represents:
options: Elements in both A and B only
All elements in either A or B or both
Elements in A but not B
Elements in neither A nor B

quizzes.text: The quadratic formula solves equations of the form:
What is the value of 5! (5 factorial)?
A function f(x) is one-to-one if:
options: It maps every x to the same y
Different inputs always produce different outputs
It's defined for all real numbers
